meaning: To make a process or action easier.
example: The new app was designed to facilitate communication between teams.

meaning: Taking action before a problem occurs rather than reacting after.
example: Being proactive about studying reduces stress before exams.

meaning: Done according to a fixed, organized plan or method.
example: A systematic approach to studying leads to better results.

meaning: To gradually gather or collect more of something over time.
example: Dust tends to accumulate on shelves if they are not cleaned regularly.

meaning: To publicly support or recommend a cause or policy.
example: She advocates for better mental health resources in schools.

meaning: To set aside or assign resources for a specific purpose.
example: The school will allocate more funds to the science department.

meaning: To work together with others toward a common goal.
example: Students were asked to collaborate on a group science project.

meaning: To develop or improve something through effort and practice.
example: You can cultivate good habits by practicing them every day.

meaning: Done consciously and intentionally; careful and unhurried.
example: She made a deliberate choice to improve her vocabulary every day.

meaning: To cause something to be set or decided in a particular way.
example: Your attitude will determine how far you go in life.

meaning: Successfully producing the desired result.
example: Exercise is an effective way to reduce stress and anxiety.

meaning: Achieving maximum productivity with minimum waste of effort or resources.
example: A good study plan makes your revision efficient and less stressful.

meaning: To improve the quality, value, or extent of something.
example: Reading regularly can enhance your understanding of the English language.

meaning: To rise and fall irregularly in number or amount.
example: Temperatures can fluctuate dramatically during the monsoon season.

meaning: To create or prepare something in a careful, systematic way.
example: They worked together to formulate a plan for the new project.

meaning: To encourage the development of something, especially over a period of time.
example: Schools should foster a love of reading in young children.

meaning: To cause something to exist or happen; to produce.
example: The new park will generate interest in outdoor activities for locals.

meaning: To put a plan or decision into action.
example: The school decided to implement a no-phone policy in classrooms.

meaning: The power to have an effect on the character, development, or behavior of someone.
example: Parents greatly influence the values their children develop.

meaning: To keep something in its existing state; to continue doing something.
example: It is important to maintain a healthy lifestyle throughout your life.

meaning: To reduce something to the smallest possible amount or degree.
example: Good preparation can help minimize mistakes during a presentation.

meaning: To notice or perceive something and register it as significant.
example: Scientists carefully observe nature before drawing conclusions.

meaning: To strengthen or support an idea, feeling, or habit.
example: Daily practice helps reinforce what you have already learned.

meaning: To find a solution to a problem; to decide firmly.
example: They worked together to resolve the misunderstanding peacefully.

meaning: To create a model or imitation of a real situation for practice or testing.
example: The software can simulate real weather conditions for training pilots.

meaning: To make a thorough and dramatic change in form, appearance, or character.
example: Consistent reading can transform your vocabulary over time.

meaning: Done by personal choice and without being forced.
example: Participation in the after-school program is completely voluntary.
